
\begin{resumo}[Abstract]

\begin{otherlanguage*}{english}

\setlength\parindent{0pt}

Annually, an average of 700,000 deaths associated with arboviruses are recorded. In urban settings, mosquito-borne arboviruses represent an escalating public health challenge, particularly in Brazil, requiring entomological surveillance strategies that integrate distribution and abundance data to better guide control measures. This study aimed to monitor Culicidae (Diptera) vectors in the municipality of Sertanópolis, northern Paraná, through ovitraps and adult captures, tracking population fluctuations and investigating viral presence (DENV, ZIKV, and CHIKV). Activities were conducted between October 2023 and December 2025 in the urban area. A total of 2,587 georeferenced ovitraps were analyzed, totaling over 180,000 eggs, which were used to calculate the Egg Density Index (EDI) and the Ovitrap Positivity Index (OPI). Monthly infestation reports based on OPI and EDI data were produced to support municipal endemic disease control agents. Results demonstrated that the OPI remained above 50, and EDI fluctuations followed climatic variations, with density peaks in December and January and declines during winter periods. The captures of adult culicid were performed using adapted Nasci aspirators at eight distinct sites, including three primary healthcare units, resulting in the collection of 11,352 individuals. Viral detection for DENV, ZIKV, and CHIKV was performed on captured {\emph{Aedes}} ({\emph{Stegomyia}}) {\emph{aegypti}} (Linnaeus, 1762) females, however, viral RNA was not detected in the samples. During the sampling period, 4 cases of CHIKV and 1,351 probable cases of DENV were notified, with the circulation of serotypes DENV-1, DENV-2, and DENV-3. It is concluded that integrated monitoring through ovitraps and adult capture is a viable and relevant model for small and medium-sized municipalities, strengthening entomological surveillance and aiding the fight against urban arboviruses, as advocated by the National Guidelines for the Prevention and Control of Urban Arboviruses.

\vspace{1cm}

\textbf{Keywords: }
{{< var tcc.palavra_chave_1_ingles >}}.
{{< var tcc.palavra_chave_2_ingles >}}.
{{< var tcc.palavra_chave_3_ingles >}}.
{{< var tcc.palavra_chave_4_ingles >}}.
{{< var tcc.palavra_chave_5_ingles >}}.

\end{otherlanguage*}
\end{resumo}
